title: Construction Act Adjudication: Four Years in Review
content:
## Construction Act Adjudication: Four Years in Review
Adjudication is a relatively new form of legislated dispute resolution mandated by recent amendments to the Ontario Construction Act . Join us for a discussion on its applications for those involved in construction projects in Ontario.
This presentation will provide a practical overview of adjudication law and procedure, as well as strategies to consider when drafting construction and project contracts to provide procedural protection and advantages in future disputes.
- Andrew Kalamut , Partner, Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group, Toronto
- Ivan Merrow , Associate, Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group, Toronto
For questions about this event, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca .
This program qualifies for up to 1 hour of CPD credit under the mandatory education regimes in British Columbia and Ontario.
Barreau du Québec Continuing Education: This program contains up to 1 hour of content. For more information regarding the declaration and recognition of educational activities, please contact the Barreau du Québec.
Note: This will take place online via webinar only. Login details will be shared in advance. This presentation will be given in English only.
Time: 12:00 p.m. - 1:00 p.m. ET
